title: Evangelion 3D
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion 3.D             Quote Post by Rei`s Pimp AKA lil`Von » Fri Apr 02, 2010 9:01 am If you werent able to figure it out from the topic sentence. what would you all think of 3.0 or 4.0 in 3D.

[]:             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Fri Apr 02, 2010 9:09 am I really do not like the 3D gimmick. So i would probably not like it so much if i HAD to watch it in 3D.

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Fri Apr 02, 2010 9:18 am Something something 3D PIG DISGUSTING something something.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Fri Apr 02, 2010 9:41 am I wouldn't like seeing it in 3D.

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Fri Apr 02, 2010 9:57 am hurr durr 3D hurr durr.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Apr 02, 2010 10:43 am If the other Rebuild films weren't in 3D I don't see why Q and Final would be. I do like what's been done with CGI, but 3D in the movies just wouldn't fit or work.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Fri Apr 02, 2010 10:48 am I'am no expert on the matter, but wouldn't making 3.0 and Final as 3D movies be well out of Khairax's financial reach? I mean, Rebuild is pretty big in terms of an anime project, but Khara sure ain't Lucasfilm or Weta.

[]:             Quote Post by Soul Fire » Fri Apr 02, 2010 3:06 pm Whilst I think 3D is overrated anyway and wouldnt like to see it in rebuild, I'm sure I heard a rumor that the budget for Q is much bigger than 2:0. I cant for the life of me remember which article or news item I read it in but Im sure they said that the gloves were going to come off in terms or what they were allowed to spend on the latest movie. Perhaps a shorter running length balances out the costs?

[]:             Quote Post by Eh?vangelion00 » Fri Apr 02, 2010 3:50 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: If the other Rebuild films weren't in 3D I don't see why Q and Final would be.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Apr 02, 2010 5:17 pm Wouldn't it be excessively hard to make the flat drawn parts of animation into 3D without making it look like a stereogram? With CG, you kind of get the 3D for free, but the parts that aren't (like all the people), how does that work?

[]:             Quote Post by IDontWantNoScrub » Fri Apr 02, 2010 6:03 pm Ornette wrote: Wouldn't it be excessively hard to make the flat drawn parts of animation into 3D without making it look like a stereogram? With CG, you kind of get the 3D for free, but the parts that aren't (like all the people), how does that work?

[]:             Quote Post by SenorSquiid » Sat Apr 03, 2010 1:34 am If it was good 3d like Avatar 3D or Coraline 3D then sure, but for some reason I don't trust anime producers nto to abuse such a gimmick for fan service purposes. I mean, it's Japan.

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Sat Apr 03, 2010 3:12 pm No.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sat Apr 03, 2010 6:35 pm Agreed. 3D adds NOTHING to the cinematic experience. I enjoyed AVATAR more in 2D than in 3D cause I could actually let my eyes explore Pandora. Besides, the best 3D I've seen was still in Coraline. They just didn't have the $250 million marketing budget to tell you the 3D was GLORIOUS. Anywho, it's not like anyone here but a few would get to see it in 3D. Most of us don't live in Japan. I mean yeah, if you want to be retarded, go out and buy a new 3D TV and expensive idiotic glasses to look like some 80s reject at home watching it sure, be my guest. Also, to do 3D right Khara would most likely have to scratch whatever (hopefully not little) work they've done and start a new. So you'd be pushing the film back a year or two. Nah. 3D can keep sucking.

[]:             Quote Post by SenorSquiid » Sun Apr 04, 2010 12:31 pm I want 4.0 to have a scene during 10th Impact where GNR reaches into the audience and a flash of tang is shown in 3D and we get the iimpression the auditorium is being instrumentalitized. And maybe an effect where the screen gets wavy around Misato's mouth when she chugs a beer and belches. And lots of horribly tasteless uses of the tech. Maybe they could get George Lucas to do it. Evangelion: SPECIAL EDITION and scenes from rebuild can just be haphazardly cut into the original TV animation so it's really jarring to the eye and the differences really stick out.

[]:             Quote Post by Grimmjow » Sun Apr 04, 2010 4:18 pm MIND RAPE in 3D Now you can see Shinji and Asuka go emo in 3D! Feel the power of Third Impact in 3D! It's so epic, your body will turn to mush and all that will be left are your clothes! The secret is the 3D glasses all contain Adam DNA!

[]:             Quote Post by SenorSquiid » Sun Apr 04, 2010 6:36 pm The last 20 minutes of Eva 4.0 3D: You Will (not) Pay Extra For the Glasses is an abrupt cut to Anno sitting naked in a chair, smoking a cigarette, scratching his crotch and drinking a beer while staring silently into the camera. Slowly the 3D effect kicks in and his eyes and every one of his pubic hairs slowly reach out into the audience. Then the Patented new Anno Taint Smell-O-Vision kicks in. Millions sickened and nauseated across the globe. 5 years later it will be a trope done to death in anime.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Sun Apr 04, 2010 9:12 pm How train your Dragon is making big money at the box office at the moment, and 3D does help the movie if you ask me since look at Avatar for example. Since I feel like the 3D visuals help the movie a lot. I have no idea how good 3D movies do in Japan at the box office, and I doubt 3.0 will be in 3D.

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Sun Apr 04, 2010 11:33 pm Jesus god all mighty!!! This thread is still alive after all this time? I thought this thing would have been put to rest a while ago! My thoughts on 3D are the same as everyone else's. Why do this thing in 3-D when there is no apparent need for it? A 2-D anime in 3D? Aside from the CGI models like the digital Tokyo 3, angels, and evas why need 3D? I cannot imagine how 3D would be done for a traditional 2-D animated film anyways. Now may this thread die and rest forever in the Evageeks Eva Graveyard. -__-

[]:             Quote Post by SenorSquiid » Mon Apr 05, 2010 12:54 am Electric Sachiel wrote: Jesus god all mighty!!! This thread is still alive after all this time? I thought this thing would have been put to rest a while ago! My thoughts on 3D are the same as everyone else's. Why do this thing in 3-D when there is no apparent need for it? A 2-D anime in 3D? Aside from the CGI models like the digital Tokyo 3, angels, and evas why need 3D? I cannot imagine how 3D would be done for a traditional 2-D animated film anyways. Now may this thread die and rest forever in the Evageeks Eva Graveyard. -__-

[]: Evangelion 3.D             Quote Post by Rei`s Pimp AKA lil`Von » Fri Apr 02, 2010 9:01 am If you werent able to figure it out from the topic sentence. what would you all think of 3.0 or 4.0 in 3D.

[]:             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Fri Apr 02, 2010 9:09 am I really do not like the 3D gimmick. So i would probably not like it so much if i HAD to watch it in 3D.

Article: Evangelion 3D
 4.0 3D: You Will (not) Pay Extra For the Glasses is an abrupt cut to Anno sitting naked in a chair, smoking a cigarette, scratching his crotch and drinking a beer while staring silently into the camera. Slowly the 3D effect kicks in and his eyes and every one of his pubic hairs slowly reach out into the audience. Then the Patented new Anno Taint Smell-O-Vision kicks in. Millions sickened and nauseated across the globe. 5 years later it will be a trope done to death in anime.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Sun Apr 04, 2010 9:12 pm How train your Dragon is making big money at the box office at the moment, and 3D does help the movie if you ask me since look at Avatar for example. Since I feel like the 3D visuals help the movie a lot. I have no idea how good 3D movies do in Japan at the box office, and I doubt 3.0 will be in 3D.

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Sun Apr 04, 2010 11:33 pm Jesus god all mighty!!! This thread is still alive after all this time? I thought this thing would have been put to rest a while ago! My thoughts on 3D are the same as everyone else's. Why do this thing in 3-D when there is no apparent need for it? A 2-D anime in 3D? Aside from the CGI models like the digital Tokyo 3, angels, and evas why need 3D? I cannot imagine how 3D would be done for a traditional 2-D animated film anyways. Now may this thread die and rest forever in the Evageeks Eva Graveyard. -__-
